task: I need to leave a short note for a neighbor about a small household matter, and I want it to be clear enough that they know what’s going on without having to ask follow-up questions. Could you help me draft something simple and practical for that situation?

turns.agent_response:
Of course — here are a couple of simple, practical versions you could use.

**Version 1:**
Hi [Neighbor’s Name],  
Just a quick note to let you know about [brief matter]. Everything is fine, but I wanted to make sure you were aware in case you notice anything. If anything comes up, please feel free to let me know. Thanks!

**Version 2:**
Hi [Neighbor’s Name],  
I wanted to leave a short note about [brief matter] so you’re not caught off guard. It’s nothing serious, just something practical to keep in mind. If you need anything clarified, please reach out. Thank you.

If you want, I can also make it:
- a little more friendly
- more formal
- or fill it in for a specific situation if you tell me what the household matter is

user_simulator.text: That’s a bit too vague. I need something I can actually use without filling in half the sentence myself. Give me one plain version I could leave on a counter or slide under a door — no placeholders, no extra options. Just simple and specific enough that a neighbor knows what to do.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a plain, ready-to-use version:

Hi neighbor, just a quick note to let you know that the water was turned off briefly this morning for a small repair. It should be back on now, but I wanted to mention it in case you noticed anything unusual. Thanks for your understanding.

user_simulator.text: That’s closer, but still a little too general for what I asked. I wanted one note I could actually use for a neighbor, and this is more like a sample. Give me a plain version for a specific household matter — short, clear, and something I could leave on the counter without explaining it myself.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s one plain, ready-to-use note you could leave on a counter or under a door:

Hi neighbor, just a quick note to let you know the upstairs bathroom is out of use for the moment because of a small plumbing issue. We’re taking care of it, and there’s no need for you to do anything. I just wanted to let you know in case you notice any noise or water off and on. Thanks.

user_simulator.text: Better, but I’d still want it a little more concrete. “Out of use for the moment” is fine, but I’d like the note to say what the neighbor should actually do, if anything — even if that’s just nothing. And if you can make it sound a touch more natural, less like a notice from an office, that would be better.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a more natural version:

Hi neighbor, just a quick note to let you know the upstairs bathroom is out of use right now because of a small plumbing issue. We’re taking care of it, so you don’t need to do anything. I just wanted to mention it in case you notice any noise or water being turned off and on. Thanks.

user_simulator.text:
Closer, yes. Still a little too tidy for what I meant. I wanted it to sound like an actual note somebody would leave at home, not a little announcement.

Make it more direct and plain — something like: the bathroom’s off limits for now, please don’t use it, we’re fixing the leak. Shorter would be better.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a shorter, more natural version:

Hi neighbor, the upstairs bathroom is off limits for now, so please don’t use it. We’re fixing a leak. Thanks.

user_simulator.text: Yes, that works. Short and plain enough.
